title: Reproduce a timing-dependent flaky failure
description: Amplify the interleaving with stress, scheduling jitter, and detectors until the intermittent failure becomes reliable.
body: Steps: 1. Characterize the flake: which assertion fails, how often, under what load. 2. Run the test in a tight loop with high parallelism to raise the failure rate. 3. Add scheduling pressure (more threads than cores, sleeps/yields at suspect points, a race detector) to widen the race window. 4. Once it fails reliably, capture the interleaving (logs/trace with thread ids and timestamps). 5. Form a hypothesis about the unsynchronized access and confirm with the detector. Rollback point: none needed; this is diagnostic and read-only. Validation: the failure reproduces on demand and the detector points at a specific shared access.
